summary: Author PRD §2 (Systems), §5 (Act-by-act content), §6 (Tech) in parallel from the locked §1 + current canon, into separate draft files; then verify each (faithfulness + staleness).

result.s2:
Path: /Users/raynos/projects/games/incremental-game-1/docs/prd-sections/section-2-systems.md
Line count: 890

Summary: Authored PRD §2 — Systems & Mechanics Catalog as a DRAFT (awaiting human review), cataloging 21 distinct game systems plus a review checklist, each with what-it-is / player-facing loop / rough data shape (field names only, no exact numbers — balance deferred to §4) / four-pillar ties / tier-of-introduction with fractal-incremental reveal. It is authored from the locked canon and LOCKED §1, explicitly overriding the superseded brainstorm volume (15-rung ladder, six estate stages, 18 expansion nodes, labour→combat cross-feed, permanent holding-loss) in favour of canon's lean shape (~8-rung-per-tier, E0–E2 v1, ~6–8-node cut-set, ZERO cross-feed, never-a-wipe), and honours every constraint (grounded/no-magic, mediocre-start, MC's-own-actions core loop, combat first-class at T0 earning Arms, trade ≤ ⅓, silk meibutsu, soft stamina, ≤1 ambiguity, active-only, IndexedDB save, macron romanization, names per canon).

Items flagged for the human (collected in §2.22): (1) confirm the two-shape pillar accrual + per-pillar recoverable dents + simple per-tier thresholds as the gate model; (2) confirm trade ≤ ⅓ as a verifier-checked hard invariant, not just a tuning target; (3) confirm auto-producers are strictly T3+ with v1's E0–E2 fully active-grind (no assignment/management panel); (4) confirm estate build/recruit ship as light flavour reveal-beats/roster-cards (not a management minigame) with martial scale hard-capped; (5) confirm belief-beasts live in a separate registry from grindable mobs and the single residual-ambiguity token stays at the Asagiri boundary-stone; (6) confirm the lean v1 skill set and ~4 quest types; (7) note that the Standing & Office kanji remains deferred to the §5 authenticity pass (not an open §2 question).
result.s5:
Path: /Users/raynos/projects/games/incremental-game-1/docs/prd-sections/section-5-content.md
Line count: 763

Summary: Authored PRD §5 (DRAFT, awaiting human review) as a per-tier act-by-act chapter — T0 Estate and T1 Village and T2 Region in full v1 detail, T3 Castle-town as a stub, T4 Edo as a roadmap. Each tier carries main-quest beats hitting its transition gate, the single per-tier antagonist revealed incrementally (debt → Magobei → Tomita+Akagi+Hanzaki → Kuroiwa → Echizen-ya factor + Touring Inspector), the growing estate roster/buildings (E0→E5), village/origin/rival NPC beats, grounded areas + mob rosters, and an open-ended side-quest list with inn-rumour folklore plus a binding belief→cause table. The two personal threads (lost-child Otsuru + origin family incl. father Jinpachi) resolve at T2, the dream foreshadows from T0, the succession (Munenori→Naoyuki) and the national mitate-banzuke ending (one ending + post-game; partial justice via a gimin-martyr osso) land at T4 — all kept grounded/no-magic, lean, with numbers deferred to §4 and reconciled to canon tier-numbering (brainstorm T3/T4/T5 → canon T2/T3/T4) and canon names (Otsuru not Oharu; Jinpachi not Kuranosuke; Kiku/Genza/Zenroku treated as non-canon).

Items flagged for the human: (1) the authenticity pass (Standing & Office kanji + martial/office-title hardening) is deferred to this section but not yet executed; (2) Jinpachi's reunion authored as a clean, un-stacked warm beat (not a third debt-bondage arc) — confirm tone and any Oyuki re-read; (3) Otsuru/Ohana name-proximity in the same post-town — flag for possible rename; (4) the Kuzuhara survivor/co-investigator needs a NEW invented name (superseded "Kiku"/"Genza" are not canon) — slot left for a human naming decision; (5) T3/T4 are forward-authored only — confirm the chosen T3 stub node and the parked/cut lists.
result.s6:
/Users/raynos/projects/games/incremental-game-1/docs/prd-sections/section-6-tech.md

435 lines

PRD §6 (Tech Architecture & Data Model) is drafted (marked DRAFT — awaiting review) as a buildable refinement of D-013: it specifies the Vite+TS+Vitest toolchain with a single `npm run verify` gate and a static itch.io build; the zero-DOM pure-core boundary (lint-enforced) with a concrete `core/` module layout and one-directional dependency rule; the `reduce(state,intent)`/`tick(state,dt)` contracts, the typed Intent union, and the stored-vs-computed GameState split; the one seeded RNG persisted in state; content as typed data registries with a content verifier and generate-don't-duplicate doc generation; the IndexedDB + base64 versioned minimal-state save with ordered migrations; the thin multi-screen responsive non-hover renderer (active-only); the `window.__qa` DEV play API; accessibility basics; and a table mapping it back to the four project conventions. Balance numbers are deliberately deferred to §4; canon constraints (active-only, no offline; no belief-creatures in spawn tables; trade ≤⅓; ≤1 ambiguity; no labour→combat cross-feed; one RNG) are baked into the content verifier's machine-checked invariants.

Items flagged for the human (in §6.13): (1) confirm IndexedDB over localStorage for the single autosave slot; (2) RNG algorithm + sub-stream split scheme choice (pins save reproducibility); (3) migration-policy ceiling — forward migrations + raw backup, with no guarantee across a future ground-up schema rewrite; (4) whether `tier` should be a stored committed value vs. fully re-derived from pillar thresholds on load. Section also proposes recording §6 as an elaboration of D-013 (or child ADR D-013a) rather than a new design decision.

Note: `docs/prd-sections/` did not exist and was created; the file is standalone and not yet linked from `docs/prd.md` (whose §-table still lists §6 as "not started") — wiring it into the main PRD is left for the integration pass.
result.v2:
I have a complete and thorough picture. Here is my verdict.

PASS (with minor fixes) — §2 faithfully reflects the canon's systems and constraints. Every load-bearing rule the task names is present and correctly stated. No contradictions, no scope bloat, no staleness in the load-bearing material. The issues below are minor accuracy/completeness items, not failures of fidelity.

CANON FIDELITY CONFIRMED (no action needed):
- No-magic / grounded: §2.9 (no belief-creatures in spawn tables; separate `BeliefBeast` registry, type-level enforcement), §2.13 (every rumour resolves 1:1 to human/natural cause; ≤1 ambiguity at the boundary-stone; no rite "works"). Correct.
- Mediocre-start: §2.7 ("milestones never read returning-memory/porter's-knot flags"; no cross-feed field exists), §2.8 (start near-zero, ZERO combat stat/training-rate bonus from conditioning). Correct.
- Combat is core + early + earns standing: §2.8 ("first-class core pillar from T0", R3 reveal, earns Arms). Correct.
- 4-pillar Influence w/ achievement-jump + seasonal-judged accrual + SIMPLE thresholds: §2.16 captures both accrual shapes, new-high-water-mark only, per-pillar recoverable dents/never-a-wipe, simple per-tier required pillars, and explicitly states "no floor/overflow fields exist" (floor+overflow REJECTED). Correct.
- Active-only / no offline: §2.2, §2.5, §2.14 all state it. Correct.
- No people-management sim: §2.5 ("no assignment/management panel … no labour-gang"), §2.17 ("No `assignmentPanel`/`labourGang` types … their absence is the canon guard"). Correct.
- MC's-own-actions loop: §2.6, §2.8, system inventory all centre the MC's own grind. Correct.
- Lean / immediate-vs-later: §2.9 (~5 mobs), §2.12 (~4 quest types, rest parked), §2.7 (lean core skills), §2.17 (E0–E2 v1, E3–E5 parked), §2.5 (auto-producers T3+). Correct.
- Trade ≤⅓, meibutsu = silk, soft stamina, martial hard-cap, macrons, IndexedDB, text+emoji+CSS: all present and correct.

ISSUES (concrete fixes):

1. OMISSION — Marriage / adoption-into-higher-status lever is absent from §2. Canon §G and §1.7.1 lock it as a REAL lean late-game (T3/T4) Standing & Office / Name & Honour lever and a castle-town takeover route. §2 mentions "alliances sealed" generically in the 2.16 pillar table but never catalogs the marriage/adoption system itself. It is a distinct system that earns two pillars and is a takeover route — it belongs in §2 (even if T3+/parked, like auto-producers 2.5 and banzuke 2.18). FIX: add a short subsection (or fold into 2.15 Factions or 2.16) cataloging marriage/adoption as a parked T3/T4 alliance/status lever feeding Standing & Office + Name & Honour, explicitly "NOT a relationship/people-management sim," with a one-line data shape and an (e) "T3+ parked" note. Currently a reader of §2 alone would not know this system exists.

2. MINOR INVENTION/CONTRADICTION — §2.8(a) states the first fight is "survived by luck / Jūbei's arrival, never skill." §1 R3 (prd.md:220) and the Jūbei cast entry (prd.md:477) establish the sequence as: survive the wolf, THEN beg Jūbei for drills ("Gates the entire training… after the humbling first fight"). Canon §E says only "survive it." So "Jūbei's arrival" as the survival mechanism is a §2 fabrication that mildly reverses the §1 ordering (Jūbei trains you after you've already survived, he doesn't save you). FIX: change to "survived by luck / sheer stubbornness, never skill" (or just "barely survived"), dropping "Jūbei's arrival" to stay consistent with §1's survive-then-seek-Jūbei beat.

3. MINOR STALENESS — §2 header (line 19 of prd.md governs this, but §2's own intro at lines 5-8) cites §1 as "LOCKED §1." §1's actual current status in prd.md is "DRAFT — integrated (staleness fixed); awaiting review" (prd.md:19, 31), and the §2 banner itself correctly says "DRAFT — awaiting human review." Calling the source "LOCKED §1" overstates §1's status (it is drafted/awaiting-review, not ADR-locked). FIX: change "the LOCKED §1 of this PRD" / "LOCKED §1" references to "the integrated §1 (awaiting review)" or "drafted §1" to avoid asserting a lock that has not been recorded as an ADR.

4. NIT (optional) — §2.6(d) and §2.16 attribute "alliances sealed" to Standing & Office but neither links it to the marriage/adoption mechanism (see issue 1); once that system is added, cross-reference it here so "alliances sealed" has a concrete source system rather than floating.

None of issues 2–4 are contradictions of a locked constraint; issue 1 is the only genuine omission of a canon-locked system. Recommend addressing issue 1 (add the marriage/adoption lever) and issue 2 (the Jūbei-survival wording) before §3; issues 3–4 are polish.
result.v5:
The plain-ASCII hits are all legitimate (the only "Tōkichi/Yagōemon/Jūbei/etc." plain-ASCII occurrences are in the "never X" guard lines or the verifier's own note text), and macron forms are used consistently in body text. Let me do a final targeted check on the mediocre-start / no-power and lost-child-girl-who-ran framing, plus T0 antagonist not conspiracy-linked.

The analysis is complete. Here is my verdict.

---

PASS (with non-blocking flags)

§5 faithfully honours canon and the locked §1. Every load-bearing constraint is present and correctly applied. The contradictions I checked for are absent; the issues below are minor polish/staleness items and author-judgment calls, none of which break canon.

PER-CONSTRAINT VERIFICATION (all PASS)

1. Grounded / no-magic — PASS. Every omen resolves to a human/natural cause. Belief→cause tables are present and binding (T0–T1 table at lines 354–361; T2 additions at 548–551). "No belief-creatures in spawn tables" is restated at T0 (187–188), T1 (319 roster is all mundane), and T2 (525–527). Yokai are INVESTIGATE-then-confront one-shots, never grindable mobs. The ≤1 ambiguity cap is held to exactly one token (the Asagiri boundary-stone, 313–314, 344, 551) with the invented "T4 castle-town ghost-story wink" explicitly deleted (641).

2. Per-tier antagonist ladder + per-tier story gates — PASS. One antagonist per tier, each revealed incrementally: T0 Inherited Debt/Tōkichi (113–130), T1 Magobei (251–265), T2 Tomita+Akagi with Hanzaki (426–445), T3 Kuroiwa (593–605), T4 Echizen-ya Sōbei + the Inspector (691–698). Matches §1.11 table exactly. T0 is correctly villain-less and NOT conspiracy-linked — the rigged-box motif is "explicitly ABSENT" from T0 (129–130). Every transition gate matches canon §B verbatim (T0→T1 67, T1→T2 213–216, T2→T3 376–378, T3→T4 570–571).

3. Lost-child = a girl who ran (Otsuru) — PASS. The truth is correct: Tama was a girl (gender-drift = the fair clue) who ran from a violent stepfather and a near-sale for debt; alive/grown as Otsuru in the post-town; the MC is not her; resolution grounded and partial (411–415, 488–490). "Otsuru (never Oharu)" is locked at line 14.

4. Origin family incl. father Jinpachi — PASS. Full origin cast present and resolving at T2 (471–490): Jinpachi (father), Oyuki, Okimi, Denbei, Kanta, Ohana. "Jinpachi (never Kuranosuke)" locked at 14; the rename rationale carried (473). Optional T4 emotional callback, no second mechanical payoff (624, 707–708).

5. Mediocre-start (no power from reveals) — PASS. Humbling near-fatal first fight authored as one-lucky-blow-then-thrashed, surviving only by luck/Jūbei (90–93). Porter's-knot and dream tagged ZERO bonus throughout (81, 87–88, 195–196). The dream rule (access only, never retroactive gift) is restated at the T2 payoff (420–424). No-labour→combat cross-feed is implicit in the conditioning framing; reveals grant access, not stats.

6. v1 = T0–2 full / T3 stub / T4 roadmap — PASS. T0–T2 authored in full; T3 explicitly a STUB cliff-hanger with the single buildable node = Porter-Guild first-contact (557–561); T4 explicitly ROADMAP/authored-forward (650–654). Matches canon §I.

7. Open-ended quests — PASS. Each tier's side-quests are framed "a suggestion + a story found in the world," not waypoint chains (192, 331–334). The ~4 v1 quest types (PEST CONTROL/HUNT/CLEAR/DEFEND) and ~5-mob budget are respected (mob tables at 181–186, 321–325, 516–523).

8. Lean — PASS. Parked-vs-cut buckets are maintained (T2/T3 parked lists 467–468, 643–646; cut list 645–646). Ladders are ~8 rungs; cut-set ~6–8 nodes; hard caps honoured (two valleys 401/509; 2–3-man retinue 406/410).

NON-BLOCKING FLAGS (polish / human-judgment, not canon violations)

- F1 (minor staleness, cosmetic): §5's status header (line 3) says "DRAFT — awaiting human review," and the PRD index (prd.md line 23) still lists §5 as "not started." Once §5 is in review, update the index row to match. Fix: set prd.md §5 status to "DRAFT — awaiting review."

- F2 (author-judgment, already self-flagged): The [FLAG-HUMAN] items (478–479 Jinpachi tone, 490 Otsuru/Ohana name-proximity, 495–498 Kuzuhara survivor NPC, 741 + 745–763 authenticity pass). These are correctly surfaced rather than silently decided — consistent with canon's "exact kanji deferred to the §5 authenticity pass" (canon §D, §I). No fix needed; they are the legitimate human queue. Note the authenticity pass (Standing & Office kanji; martial-title hardening) is acknowledged-but-not-executed (738–741, 747–750) — that is canon-compliant deferral, not an omission.

- F3 (very minor, optional): The locked §1 §1.5.1 region-ladder example uses "broker of the post-town trade"; §5 (387–390) reproduces it faithfully but flags exact rung copy as §3's job — correct boundary discipline. No action.

- F4 (consistency nit): §5 line 254 writes the doctored measuring-box as kyō-masu (macron) — correct; canon and §1 agree. No drift found anywhere; all other names (Tōkichi, Yagōemon, Jūbei, Sōbei, Tōge-mura, Saemon-no-jō, yōnin, karō) use proper-Hepburn macrons consistently. The only plain-ASCII occurrences are inside "never X" guard clauses and the verifier's own notes (Oharu/Kuranosuke/Ren/Kiku/Genza), which is correct usage.

No canon contradictions found. §5 is internally consistent with both the locked-decisions canon and locked §1, including the subtle items (T0 racket-absent, decoupled personal-vs-house ceiling at chief steward/yōnin, partial-justice/gimin-osso, no-reset, marriage lever as T3/T4 Standing/Name route, silk meibutsu under the trade ≤⅓ cap).

- /Users/raynos/projects/games/incremental-game-1/brainstorms/2026-06-25-locked-decisions.md (canon, no changes needed)
result.v6:
All the draft's concepts (scrolls, rake rice, attributes, cold open) are well-grounded in either canon or the cited mechanics-architecture sketch. The draft is consistent with its sources. Let me do a final check on a couple of subtle canon points: the seasonal accrual phrasing and whether the "tier stored" flag in §6.4 has any canon tension, plus verify D-013's exact wording.

I have everything I need. Here is my assessment.

PASS

§6 is accurate, internally consistent, and faithfully reflects all nine required items. It refines the locked D-013 (canon §H, locked-decisions line 217) without contradicting it, and correctly defers balance numbers to §4. All concepts I spot-checked that aren't in the locked-decisions canon (lore scrolls / `read_scroll`, "rake the rice", the cold-open kura, the STR/AGI/INT/SPD attribute set) are grounded in the cited sources (the mechanics-and-architecture sketch and canon §G/§H), so nothing is invented or stale.

Required-item coverage (all present and correct):
1. Pure-core no-DOM boundary — §6.2 (zero DOM/canvas/window in `src/core/`, one-directional deps, ESLint boundary rule as build failure). Correct.
2. One seeded RNG — §6.7 + §6.3 + §6.1 (single RNG in `GameState`, pure `next`, child-stream splitting, `Math.random()` lint-banned). Correct.
3. Vite + TS + Vitest — §6.1 (plus ESLint/Prettier, strict TS, `npm run verify` gate). Correct.
4. IndexedDB save + export + versioned minimal-state — §6.8 + §6.4 (single autosave, base64 export/import, store-only-non-derivable, ordered migrations). Correct, matches canon §H line 191.
5. Responsive desktop+mobile, not hover-dependent — §6.9 + §6.11 (fluid reflow, every value reachable without hover, tap/focus equivalents). Correct.
6. Active-only — §6.3, §6.8, §6.9, §6.12 (abstract `dtTicks`, no offline-accrual code path, backgrounding pauses). Correct, matches canon §H line 176.
7. Multi-screen progressive reveal — §6.9 (real multi-screen shell, nav/screens revealed by unlock predicates, appears single-screen early). Correct, matches canon §H line 182-183.
8. Data-as-code + generate-docs — §6.5 (typed registries in `core/content`) + §6.6 (content verifier + `gen-docs.ts --check` fails build on drift). Correct, satisfies the generate-don't-duplicate convention mechanically.
9. DEV play API — §6.10 (`window.__qa`, DEV-only/dead-code-eliminated, drives the real typed intents). Correct, matches the playtest-by-code convention and the capture-game-states skill.

Minor issues (non-blocking — all are quality nits, fixes suggested; none is a contradiction with canon):

1. [Minor / consistency] §6.4 stored-`combat?` comment. Line 199 calls it "also derived-ish but stored mid-fight for save-resume." This is a slight contradiction with the §6.4 cardinal rule ("store only what is NOT derivable"). A live encounter's seeded roll-state is genuinely non-derivable mid-fight (it depends on RNG already consumed), so it belongs in stored state — the "derived-ish" aside undersells that. Fix: change the comment to "non-derivable while a fight is live (carries the encounter's consumed RNG/positions); absent otherwise" to remove the apparent rule-violation. Already flagged for the human as item 4's sibling — worth tightening the prose regardless.

2. [Minor / canon-precision] §6.3 per-season bullet (line 156) says the seasonal judged Influence results fire "only on a new high-water mark." Canon §D (lines 90-92) says accrual = "achievement JUMPS + seasonal JUDGED RESULTS … the seasonal appraisal fires PER SEASON (4×/in-game year)." The appraisal *fires every season*; only the *up-only pillar increase* is gated to a new high-water mark. The draft conflates "appraisal fires" with "value rises." Fix: reword to "the seasonal appraisal fires every season; it raises a pillar only on a new high-water mark (up-only)" so it matches canon's "never a passive time-trickle" without implying the appraisal itself skips seasons. (§6.6's machine-check bullet "no Influence path is a passive per-tick trickle or flat per-action increment" is correct and should stay.)

3. [Minor / omission] Romanization-macrons lock (canon §H line 180, LOCKED project-wide) is not asserted anywhere in §6, even though §6.5 defines the content/display registries (`resources.ts` display/emoji, `dialogues.ts` text) where ASCII-slip enforcement would live. Not strictly a "tech architecture" item, but §6.6's content verifier is the natural mechanical home for a macron/no-plain-ASCII lint over display strings, exactly parallel to the canon-invariant checks it already lists. Fix: add one bullet to §6.6's machine-check list — "display/name strings use proper-Hepburn macrons (no plain-ASCII romaji slips), canon §H" — or explicitly note it's deferred to the §5 authenticity pass. Low priority; flag, don't block.

4. [Minor / omission] Abbreviated K/M/B number formatting (canon §H line 178-179, LOCKED) is unmentioned. It is a renderer/display concern that fits §6.9 (art register / display) in one line. Fix: add to §6.9 — "large values render with abbreviated K/M/B formatting (canon §H), human-scaled, not scientific/myriad." Minor.

Over-engineering check: none found. The module list maps 1:1 to already-locked systems (§6.12 makes this claim and it holds); the verify gate, registries, and `__qa` surface are all directly mandated by the four CLAUDE.md conventions, not speculative. The four "Flagged for the human" items (§6.13) are appropriately scoped as confirmations/reversible choices, not new design — consistent with the draft's correct framing of §6 as an elaboration of D-013 (proposed D-013a) rather than a new decision.
